_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications_uncertain: The document describes general indications for tricuspid valve surgery (stenosis and regurgitation) but does not provide specific clinical indications or criteria for 'immediate reoperation'.

preauth.label: Clinical notes indicating need for reoperation

claim.label: Post procedure stills of ECHO with report

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detailed history, signs & symptoms, indication for procedure?
Clinical notes indicating need for reoperation?
Was the Echo/ Doppler report suggestive of Tricuspid Stenosis/ Tricuspid Regurgitation?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Does the Post procedure still of ECHO show repair/ replacement of the valve?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary mentioning date of follow-up submitted?
Does the discharge summary mention need for reoperation?
Is the barcode of implant used submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes not indicating the need for reoperation
Echo/Doppler report not suggestive of Tricuspid Stenosis or Tricuspid Regurgitation
Post-procedure ECHO stills not showing repair/replacement of the valve
Barcode of implant not submitted when an implant was used
Discharge summary not submitted, missing date of follow-up, or not mentioning the need for reoperation

clinical_key_pointers: Tricuspid Stenosis (TS) causes: Rheumatic heart disease (often with mitral stenosis), large vegetations in infective endocarditis, carcinoid syndrome, systemic diseases (SLE, APLA syndrome), permanent pacing and ICD leads causing sub-valvular structures, benign tumors (atrial myxomas).

TS clinical features: Reduced exertional capacity, fatigue, exertional syncope, hepatic congestion, leg edema, ascites, deterioration of liver function tests, anasarca. Physical exam may show 'Giant a waves' in JVP, 'slow y descent', low frequency presystolic mid-diastolic murmur at lower left sternal border (4th intercostal space). Lungs are clear in isolated TS.

Tricuspid Regurgitation (TR) causes: Primary (intrinsic valvular apparatus abnormalities) or secondary (right ventricular dilatation, annular dilation, leaflet tethering due to RV pressure/volume overload, more common).

TR clinical features: Right-sided heart failure symptoms (painful hepatosplenomegaly, ascites, peripheral edema), pulsations in neck from distended and pulsatile jugular veins, exercise intolerance. Physical exam may show jugular venous distension with prominent V wave (increases with inspiration - Kussmaul's sign), S3 gallop (dilated RV), pansystolic murmur (high pitched, loudest 4th intercostal space parasternal, increases with inspiration, exercise, leg raising; decreases with standing, Valsalva). Also ascites, cachexia, jaundice, atrial fibrillation, peripheral edema, RV heave, S4 gallop (increases with inspiration). Underlying condition features like pulmonary hypertension or infective endocarditis may also be present.
